settings.heading: Jon Sarkin (1953–2024)
settings.text: Jon Sarkin was an outsider artist whose prolific output spanned three decades following a catastrophic stroke in 1989. Working from his studio in Gloucester, Massachusetts, he produced an estimated twenty thousand drawings, paintings, collages, and mixed-media works before his death in July 2024. His practice has been the subject of peer-reviewed scholarship, a bestselling biography, and international exhibition.

settings.heading: Advisory Board
settings.text:
Colin Rhodes — Distinguished Professor; Contributing Editor, Raw Vision
Tony Millionaire — Eisner Award–winning cartoonist
Dr. Alice Flaherty — Harvard Medical School / Massachusetts General Hospital

settings.heading: The Estate
settings.text:
The Jon Sarkin Estate manages the artist's legacy, including the catalog raisonné, exhibition loans, and licensing through Artists Rights Society (ARS), New York.

For inquiries about Jon Sarkin's work, contact Mark Henderson, Estate Manager, at art@jonsarkin.com.
